summary: On Jan 3, 2026, US launched Operation Absolute Resolve - bombing Venezuelan military bases, capturing President Maduro. 55+ military deaths (32 Cuban, 23 Venezuelan), 2 civilians. Maduro arraigned in Manhattan federal court Jan 5. VP Rodriguez sworn in as acting president. UNSC convened Jan 5 but no UNGA action as of Jan 11. International response divided: condemned by Brazil, Chile, China, France, Mexico, Russia, Spain; praised by Argentina, Germany, Israel, Peru, Ukraine. UN experts called it 'international crime of aggression'. No ESS called after 8 days.
content: Comprehensive research on the US military operation in Venezuela and UNGA resolution prospects. Attack: Jan 3, 2026, codename Operation Absolute Resolve. US bombed military infrastructure across northern Venezuela, Delta Force captured Maduro at Fort Tiuna. Maduro flown to NYC, arraigned Jan 5. VP Rodriguez became acting president Jan 5. Negotiations ongoing - political prisoners being released. US Senate voted 52-47 on war resolution limiting Trump's powers Jan 8. UNSC convened Jan 5 at request of China, Colombia, Russia - no resolution due to US veto power. NO UNGA Emergency Special Session called as of Jan 11 (8 days post-attack). Historical precedent: Grenada condemned in 8 days, Panama in 9 days (both during regular UNGA sessions). Iraq 2003 was NEVER condemned by UNGA despite massive opposition. No precedent for ESS against a US operation.
